steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Identify the vertices and diagonal structure
steps.content:
The figure shows a square with vertices at C(0, 5), D(5, 0), A(0, −5), and B(−5, 0).

Since the diagonals lie on the coordinate axes, they are perpendicular and bisect each other at the origin.

The diagonals divide the square into 4 congruent right triangles, each with legs extending 5 units from the center to a vertex on the nearest axis.

steps.title: Calculate the area using triangle decomposition
steps.content:
Each right triangle has legs of length 5 and 5, so its area is:
$$\text{Area of one triangle} = \frac{1}{2} \times 5 \times 5 = 12.5 \text{ square units}$$
The total area of the square is the sum of all 4 triangles:
$$\text{Total area} = 4 \times 12.5 = 50 \text{ square units}$$
That is the correct answer.
